review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-098-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: packages/domain/src/ui-surfaces.js (createCoreDomainUiState error_message_copy)
findings.description: "error" is declared in CORE_DOMAIN_UI_STATE_CONTRACT.states and the JSON contract.states, but is excluded from state_matrix_required_states and the matrix. For state=="error" (and "loading") the error_message_copy falls through to "Synthetic UI state is ready.", a semantic mismatch for a field named error_message_copy. Display-only; no boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: Either add an explicit error/loading copy branch or rename the field to status_copy; harmless as-is.

findings.title: packages/domain/src/ui-surfaces.js (createCoreDomainUiSurfaceStateMatrix, validateCoreDomainCp098Coverage)
findings.description: The required-state list [loading,empty,denied,review_required,ready] is duplicated as inline literals in two functions and is not sourced from a shared field. CORE_DOMAIN_UI_STATE_CONTRACT (JS) also omits the state_matrix_required_states key present in the JSON contract, so there are two notions of "states". Drift risk is mitigated by tests pinning matrix length=15.
findings.recommendation: Derive matrix/coverage from a single state_matrix_required_states constant mirrored from the JSON contract.

findings.title: scripts/validate-rp01-core-domain-contract.mjs (ui_state_contract assertions)
findings.description: Validator asserts writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, states, and responsive_modes on ui_state_contract, but does not assert accepts_real_client_data:false, permission_badge_policy:"reference_only_never_evaluated", audit_hint_policy:"display_only_never_written", fixture_policy:"synthetic_only", nor cross-check ui_state_contract.surfaces against CORE_DOMAIN_UI_SURFACES. The JSON values are correct and coverage checks fixture.uses_real_client_data, so the boundary holds; the script just under-asserts it.
findings.recommendation: Add assertions for the declared no-real-data / reference-only policy flags and surface set to harden the boundary the pack guarantees.

notes: Boundaries hold across all four focus areas. Permission references are reference-only (createCoreDomainPermissionBadge sets evaluated:false; effect derived solely from the requested state string, never from a real permission check) and never imply approval (non-denied/review states show label "Reference only"). Audit hints are display-only (display_only:true, writes_audit_event:false, audit_event_id null). All UI state objects, fixtures, evidence, review, and handoff set writes_product_state:false, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, writes_audit_event:false, synthetic_only/uses_real_client_data:false; fixtures come from createCoreDomainSyntheticFixtureSet only. No DB/IO writes; all objects deep-frozen. Responsive modes desktop_dense/mobile_single_column produce distinct layout metadata (mobile min_touch_target_px 44, max_columns 1). LDIP untouched (ldip_implemented:false; hrx_boundary is a descriptive string only). Contract/JS/validator/tests are mutually consistent: 3 surfaces x 5 required states = 15 (matrix, fixture, evidence, coverage all 15); states and responsive_modes cross-validated; prior current_pack CP00-097 correctly demoted to api_pack and re-validated; schema bumped v0.4->v0.5; handoff points to CP00-099 / RP01.P04.M05.S05. Surfaces map to micro-phases M03/M04/M05; no surface maps to M02 even though range starts at RP01.P04.M02.S04 — consistent with M02.S04 being the contract/inventory subphase (CP00-097 handoff ended at RP01.P04.M02.S03), so treated as intentional, not a defect. No P0/P1/P2; remaining items are low-severity hardening/consistency nits. Approve.
